# LiveChat

> LiveChat is a Discord bot paired with a stream overlay that lets friends display images, play videos, or trigger sounds live on a stream, via a simple Discord command (/livechat). Inspired by the streamers group Cacabox, it makes streams interactive by letting friends send media that appears directly on the streamer's screen.

LiveChat natively supports Discord, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram Reels, Twitter/X, Tenor, and Giphy, as well as direct file uploads. It works with OBS Studio, Streamlabs, PRISM Live, and any software supporting browser sources. The project is open source under the GPL-3.0 license and supports self-hosting.

## Features

- Easy media sharing from Discord, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram Reels, Twitter/X, Tenor, Giphy, or file upload
- Send media simultaneously to all connected streamers on the same Discord server
- Add meme-style overlay text (Impact font) on top of media
- Built-in proxy that protects streamers' IP addresses by relaying media through the server
- Compatible with any streaming software that supports browser sources (OBS, Streamlabs, PRISM Live)

## Pages

- [Home](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/): demo, features, and setup steps
- [Configuration](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/config): add the Discord bot and generate the overlay link
- [Usage](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/usage): how to use the bot
- [Updates](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/updates): release notes
- [Privacy](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/privacy): privacy policy
- [Terms](https://livechat.nevylish.fr/terms): terms of service

## Technical resources

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/Nevylish/LiveChat): full source code, self-hosting instructions (Node.js, pnpm, Docker)
- License: GPL-3.0
